parts.title: Refrigerator Door Shelf Bin
parts.installSteps:
1) Open the refrigerator door and find the cracked or missing bin.
2) Lift the old bin straight up off the molded rail on the inside of the door.
3) Line the new bin up with the rail's tabs and push straight down until it seats — no tools required.
parts.shipEta: Ships today if ordered by 3 PM ET

repairStories.title: Cracked gallon bin — 30-second swap
repairStories.body: Our old bin had split down the side after years of holding a full gallon of milk. Popped the old one off the door rail, slid the new one on. Didn't even need to move anything else. Literally under a minute.
repairStories.author: Mike R.

repairStories.title: Replaced two yellowed bins at once
repairStories.body: Both of our door bins had turned yellow and one was cracked. Ordered two. Hardest part was figuring out which direction the tabs seat — they only go on one way, so just line it up and push down firmly. Fridge instantly looks newer.
repairStories.author: Sandra L.

repairStories.title: Fit perfectly on an older Kenmore
repairStories.body: Fridge is a Kenmore about 12 years old. Was nervous the new bin wouldn't fit, but it snapped right onto the door rail. Exact OEM match. Packaging was great too — no damage in shipping.
repairStories.author: Greg B.

repairStories.title: Kid broke it, I fixed it
repairStories.body: My daughter yanked on the door bin and cracked the end off. Thought I'd have to call a repair tech. Turned out I could just lift the old one off and drop the new one on. Felt like a hero for about 20 seconds.
repairStories.author: Priya S.

repairStories.title: Fix for one bin, precaution for the other
repairStories.body: The bottom bin cracked so I replaced it. Figured the top bin was the same age and would go soon — bought a second as a spare. Took the whole project 5 minutes. Glad I didn't wait for it to fail mid-grocery-haul.
repairStories.author: Ravi K.

repairStories.title: Matched our fridge exactly
repairStories.body: Side-by-side Whirlpool. The replacement bin was identical to what came out — same dimensions, same tab pattern, same clear plastic. No ambiguity, no comparison needed. Took maybe 10 seconds to install once it was out of the box.
repairStories.author: Tom H.

questions.author: Derek
questions.question: Will this bin fit a Kenmore side-by-side or is it Whirlpool-only?
questions.answer: PS11752778 is a Whirlpool part (OEM WPW10321304) and is used across several Whirlpool-family brands — Whirlpool, Kenmore, KitchenAid, and some Amana models share this exact bin. Always double-check with your model number before ordering.

questions.author: Jenna P.
questions.question: Do I need any tools to swap the old bin out?
questions.answer: No tools required. Open the refrigerator door, lift the damaged bin straight up off the molded rail, then line the new bin up with the tabs and push down until it seats. The whole swap is usually under a minute.

questions.author: Emma
questions.question: My old bin is yellowed but not cracked — is the new one clear?
questions.answer: Yes, the replacement is a new clear plastic bin. Yellowing is a cosmetic age effect on the original bin; the replacement ships clear and stays clear for years under normal use.

questions.author: Alicia
questions.question: One of the molded tabs on my door rail snapped off — will the bin still stay in?
questions.answer: The bin relies on multiple tabs for support, so one missing tab usually still holds. If two or more tabs are broken, the bin will sag under a full gallon — at that point the door liner itself may need replacing, not just the bin.

questions.author: Marcus
questions.question: What's the difference between PS11752778 and the older part numbers like 2198449?
questions.answer: They're the same physical part. 2198449 (and 2171046, 2171047, 2198450, etc.) are older Whirlpool part numbers that have all been superseded by WPW10321304 / PS11752778. Ordering PS11752778 will get you the current-production version of the same bin.

questions.author: Lisa C.
questions.question: Will this fit my WDT780SAEM1?
questions.answer: No — WDT780SAEM1 is a dishwasher model number, and PS11752778 is a refrigerator door bin. You'd want to search using your refrigerator's model number (usually on a sticker inside the fridge, along the left wall or ceiling).

parts.title: Refrigerator Ice Maker Assembly
parts.installSteps:
1) Unplug the refrigerator and remove the ice maker cover.
2) Disconnect the wiring harness and unscrew the assembly.
3) Mount the new assembly, reconnect the harness, restore power.
parts.shipEta: Ships today if ordered by 3 PM ET

repairStories.title: Fixed in an afternoon — no appliance experience needed
repairStories.body: Our ice maker had been dead for two months. Watched one YouTube video, ordered this part. Unplugged the fridge, pulled off the ice maker cover, disconnected the harness, swapped the unit, plugged it back in. Had ice within 24 hours. Total hands-on time maybe 30 minutes.
repairStories.author: Dan M.

repairStories.title: Saved $350 vs. a service call
repairStories.body: Repairman quoted $400. Part was $128 and took me 45 minutes. The hardest part was reaching the wire harness clips — a small flathead helps. Everything clicked back together perfectly and the ice maker started cycling the same night.
repairStories.author: Rachel T.

repairStories.title: Check the water valve first — mine was the valve, not this
repairStories.body: Ordered this assembly thinking it was the problem. Turned out my water inlet valve (PS734936) was clogged. Returned this unit unopened. Tip: if the ice maker makes clicking sounds but no water fills, start with the valve — cheaper fix.
repairStories.author: Chris L.

questions.author: Mark S.
questions.question: How do I know if it's the ice maker assembly or the water inlet valve that's bad?
questions.answer: Listen: if you hear clicking but no water fills the mold, the water inlet valve (PS734936) is usually the culprit. If water fills but no ice harvests (or the module doesn't cycle at all), replace the ice maker assembly (PS11738120). Valve is cheaper — start there.

questions.author: Sarah K.
questions.question: Does this fit the WRS325SDHZ?
questions.answer: Yes — PS11738120 is compatible with the Whirlpool WRS325SDHZ side-by-side refrigerator. Make sure the bail arm is accessible before ordering.

parts.title: Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve
parts.installSteps:
1) Shut off the water supply and unplug the refrigerator.
2) Disconnect the inlet tubes and wiring harness from the valve.
3) Mount the new valve, reconnect tubes + harness, restore water and power.
parts.shipEta: Ships today if ordered by 3 PM ET

parts.title: Dishwasher Door Latch
parts.installSteps:
1) Remove the inner door panel screws along the top edge.
2) Unplug the latch wiring harness and swap in the new latch.
3) Reinstall the inner panel and test the door switch.
parts.shipEta: Ships today if ordered by 3 PM ET

compatibilities.note: WDT780SAEM1 is a Whirlpool dishwasher model; PS11752778 is a refrigerator door shelf bin. Different appliance — not compatible. Please search with your refrigerator's model number instead.
WRS325SDHZ is a Whirlpool side-by-side refrigerator that uses this door shelf bin. Snaps onto the molded rail on the inside of the door — no tools required.
PS11738120 is a direct-fit ice maker assembly for the Whirlpool WRS325SDHZ. Verify the bail arm location before starting — it's accessible from inside the freezer compartment.
The Whirlpool WRS325SDHZ uses this dual-inlet water valve for both the ice maker and the door water dispenser. Shut off the water supply before replacing.
PS11746240 is the OEM door latch assembly for the Whirlpool WDT780SAEM1 dishwasher. No special tools required beyond a T20 Torx bit for the inner panel screws.

steps: Check that the water supply line behind the fridge is connected and the shutoff valve is fully open.
Inspect the water filter — a clogged filter is the most common cause of low or no water flow. Replace if it's been over 6 months.
Hold the dispenser lever for 10–15 seconds: if you hear the valve open (a click or hum) but nothing flows, the water inlet valve (PS734936) is likely faulty.
If no click at all, check the door switch — the dispenser won't activate unless the fridge door is fully closed.

commonQuestions.question: Water trickles slowly but doesn't fully stop — is it the valve or the filter?
commonQuestions.answer: Slow trickle usually points to a partially clogged filter. Replace the filter first ($20–40) before ordering the valve ($55). If flow improves immediately after a new filter, that was the fix.

commonQuestions.question: Ice maker and water dispenser both stopped at the same time — what does that mean?
commonQuestions.answer: If both stop simultaneously, the water inlet valve (PS734936) is almost certainly the cause — it controls supply to both the ice maker fill tube and the dispenser line. Replace the valve.

steps: Make sure the door is fully latched — most dishwashers won't start if the door latch switch doesn't engage.
Check the control lock: hold the lock button for 3 seconds to disable child lock if the control panel is unresponsive.
Reset the dishwasher by cutting power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore and try a new cycle.
If the door clicks but the dishwasher still won't start, the door latch assembly (PS11746240) is likely faulty and needs replacement.

commonQuestions.question: The dishwasher clicks when I close the door but still won't run — is that the latch?
commonQuestions.answer: A click usually means the mechanical latch is catching, but the electrical switch inside may have failed. The door latch assembly (PS11746240) includes both the mechanical latch and the microswitch — replacing the whole assembly is the standard fix.

commonQuestions.question: How long does the door latch replacement take?
commonQuestions.answer: Most customers finish in 20–30 minutes. You'll remove the inner door panel (T20 Torx screws along the top edge), unplug the wiring harness, swap the latch, and reassemble. No special skills required.

steps: Confirm the bail arm is down and the ice maker is switched on.
Check the water supply valve behind the fridge and the fill tube for ice blockage.
If still no ice, note any blinking pattern on the ice maker module (service manual) before replacing the ice maker assembly.

commonQuestions.question: Ice maker clicks but no ice comes out — where do I start?
commonQuestions.answer: Clicking without fill usually points to the water inlet valve (PS734936). Clicking WITH fill but no harvest points to the ice maker module (PS11738120). Check the fill tube for ice blockage first — that's the cheapest fix.

commonQuestions.question: Is it worth replacing the whole assembly or just the module?
commonQuestions.answer: For most Whirlpool top-freezer / French-door models, the assembly (PS11738120) is sold as a complete unit — module, mold, and thermostat together. Replacing just the module is not typically a consumer repair.
